flashcards.definition: The highest exponent of x in the polynomial; determines the function's overall behavior.
The coefficient of the term with the highest degree; its sign affects end behavior.
A function of the form aₙxⁿ + … + a₁x + a₀ with non-negative integer exponents.
A point where the graph changes direction; a degree-n polynomial has at most n − 1 turning points.
Every polynomial of degree n ≥ 1 has exactly n zeros (real or complex, counting multiplicity).
